2606.24780.title: BluTrain: A C++/CUDA Framework for AI Systems
2606.24780.paper_summary: Progress in deep learning is, at scale, more a matter of systems engineering than of modelling: the behaviour of a model in training (its throughput, its memory footprint, and the numerical fidelity of the result) is determined less by the architecture itself than by how that architecture is expressed on the hardware. To achieve absolute control over this hardware expression while abstracting away systems complexity to make modelling seamless and eliminating the need for repetitive orchestration logic, BluTrain was architected from first principles as a robust, lightweight, and architecture-general training framework in standard C++ and the core CUDA programming model. Every layer is implemented natively: a typed tensor module with reverse-mode autograd, a linear-algebra library, a caching allocator, a multi-mode distributed-execution module, and an MLIR-based deep-learning compiler. In formal evaluations training a 124M-parameter GPT-2 baseline in FP32 on an 8-GPU 6000 Ada system, BluTrain outperforms industry-standard baselines in both throughput (sustaining an average of 407K tokens/s versus PyTorch's 395K tokens/s) and memory efficiency (achieving up to a 22% footprint reduction), while strictly preserving numerical fidelity and converging to a marginally lower final validation loss. With every layer explicitly open to native tuning, the performance ceiling is the framework's own to raise.
2606.24780.paper_summary_zh: 進展在深度學習上，從規模上看，更像是系統工程的問題，而非建模的問題：模型在訓練中的行為（其吞吐量、內存佔用和結果的數值精度）不僅由架構本身決定，而是由該架構在硬體上的表現所決定。為了在抽象系統複雜性的同時實現對這種硬體表現的絕對控制，使建模變得無縫並消除重複的協調邏輯需求，BluTrain從基本原則出發，設計為一個穩健、輕量且架構通用的訓練框架，使用標準C++和核心CUDA編程模型。每一層都是原生實現的：一個帶有反向模式自動微分的類型張量模塊、一個線性代數庫、一個緩存分配器、一個多模式分布式執行模塊，以及一個基於MLIR的深度學習編譯器。在正式評估中，使用8-GPU 6000 Ada系統以FP32訓練124M參數的GPT-2基準，BluTrain在吞吐量（平均維持407K tokens/s，相較於PyTorch的395K tokens/s）和內存效率（實現高達22%的佔用減少）方面超越了行業標準基準，同時嚴格保持數值精度並收斂到略低的最終驗證損失。隨著每一層明確開放給原生調整，性能上限由框架自身提升。
2606.24780.author: Adhitya Charan et.al.
2606.24780.authors: Adhitya Charan, Adwaid Suresh, Anuj Kumar, Aparna A, Dhanakumar K, Dharun M S, Dinesh G, Goutham Kumar Reddy K, Harshini V M, Jenifa D, Jona Delcy C A, Kathirvel S, Killi Uma Maheswara Rao, Kiruthik Kanna M, Kurra Vishnu Sai, Madhumithaa G K, Navin Kumar, Ram Charan Golla, Revathi T, Rishikkanth R, Sanjay Krishna M, Surendra Vendra
